title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: Every year, millions of birds (0) undertake epic journeys across continents, navigating with a precision that has long baffled scientists. This seasonal movement, known as migration, is (1) by the need to find food and suitable breeding grounds. How these creatures manage to find their way over thousands of miles of open ocean and trackless deserts is a (2) of intense research. Some species are believed to (3) use of the Earth’s magnetic field, acting as a natural compass to maintain their heading. Others (4) on celestial cues, using the positions of the stars and the sun to steer their course. Recent studies have also (5) light on the role of olfactory maps, suggesting that some birds can actually 'smell' their way home. Despite these incredible navigational skills, migration is a (6) business, with birds facing threats from predators, exhaustion, and extreme weather. Human activity has further (7) these challenges by destroying vital stopover habitats. Protecting these areas is (8) to ensuring the survival of migratory species. As we continue to uncover the secrets of avian navigation, we gain a deeper appreciation for the resilience and complexity of the natural world and the extraordinary feats of endurance that define these annual global travels.

example.explanation: “Undertake a journey” is a standard formal collocation.

questions.explanation: “Driven by” is the standard way to describe the motivation behind biological behavior.
“Subject of research” is the correct academic collocation.
“Make use of” is a fixed idiomatic phrase meaning to utilize something.
“Rely on” is the correct phrasal verb for depending on a source of information.
“Shed light on” is a fixed idiom meaning to make something clearer.
“Perilous” is a formal adjective meaning full of danger or risk.
“Compounded” means to make a problem or difficult situation worse.
“Crucial to” is used to emphasize that something is essential for success.
